patient_data.chief_complaint: Sudden severe lower abdominal pain with dizziness and vaginal spotting
symptoms: Sudden onset severe right lower abdominal pain radiating to shoulder (Kehr's sign)
Amenorrhea — last menstrual period 7 weeks ago
patient_data.history: G2P1 (1 prior vaginal delivery). History of chlamydia infection age 22 (treated). Previous IUD (removed 6 months ago for conception attempt). Irregular cycles

lab_results.hemoglobin: 8.8 g/dL (dropped from baseline 12.5 — acute blood loss)
lab_results.transvaginal_US: Empty uterus, complex right adnexal mass 3.5cm, moderate free fluid in Morison pouch and pouch of Douglas

should_detect: SURGICAL EMERGENCY — immediate gynecology/surgical consult for laparoscopy or laparotomy
Large-bore IV access x2 — aggressive fluid resuscitation with warmed crystalloid
Type and crossmatch — prepare 2-4 units packed RBCs (Hb 8.8 with ongoing bleeding)
O-negative blood if transfusion needed before crossmatch
Rh-negative patient — administer anti-D immunoglobulin (RhoGAM) 300mcg IM
Hemodynamic instability + free fluid = ruptured ectopic with active bleeding
hCG 2400 with empty uterus above discriminatory zone confirms ectopic
Risk factors: prior chlamydia (PID/tubal damage) + prior IUD use
Salpingectomy vs salpingotomy decision based on surgical findings
